characters.name: Agent Powers
characters.description: Agent PowersBackground informationFirst appearanceScary-okeLast appearanceA Tale of Two StansVoiceNick OffermanCharacter informationOccupationGovernment agentAffiliationsU.S. GovernmentAlliesAgent TriggerFateHas his mind erased along the rest of the federal agents and Trigger thanks to the modifiedmemory erasing gunQuote"Then we'll make them believe us. This is the town we've been searching for."[Source]Agent Powersis a U.S. government operative investigating the paranormal activities inGravity Falls, Oregon, alongside his partnerAgent Trigger. Powers and Trigger arrive at the Mystery Shack. In "Scary-oke," Agent Powers and his partner both arrive at theMystery Shackto investigate reports of mysterious activity in Gravity Falls, and begin an inspection of the shack's gift shop. During their visit, Powers discloses toDippertheir ongoing investigation of a paranormal case that he suspects is deeply rooted within the town, which entices Dipper to help with their research. Powers gives him a business card thatStanquickly confiscates, eventually convincing the agents that the paranormal aspects of the town are mere legends to drum up business.

characters.name: Agent Trigger
characters.description: Agent TriggerBackground informationFirst appearanceScary-okeLast appearanceA Tale of Two StansVoiceBrad AbrellInspirationJeff Rowe[1]Character informationOccupationGovernment agentAffiliationsU.S. GovernmentGoalTo stopStan'suniverse portal(formerly)AlliesAgent PowersLikesJutston's Gentleman Gel[2]FateHas his mind erased along the rest of the federal agents and Powers thanks to the modifiedmemory erasing gunQuote"I can confirm: Not funny."[Source]Agent Jeff Trigger[2]is a government operative investigating the paranormal activity inGravity Falls, Oregon, alongside his partnerAgent Powers. Trigger and his partner, Agent Powers. In "Scary-oke," Agent Trigger and his partner Agent Powers both arrive at theMystery Shackto investigate mysterious activity in Gravity Falls, and begin an inspection of the shack's gift shop. During their visit, Powers discloses toDippertheir ongoing investigation of a paranormal case that he suspects is deeply rooted within the town, which entices Dipper to help with their research. Powers gives him a business card thatStanquickly confiscates, hurriedly convincing the agents that the paranormal aspects of the town are mere legends to drum up business.

characters.name: Agent Trigger
characters.description: Agent TriggerBackground informationFirst appearanceScary-okeLast appearanceA Tale of Two StansVoiceBrad AbrellInspirationJeff Rowe[1]Character informationOccupationGovernment agentAffiliationsU.S. GovernmentGoalTo stopStan'suniverse portal(formerly)AlliesAgent PowersLikesJutston's Gentleman Gel[2]FateHas his mind erased along the rest of the federal agents and Powers thanks to the modifiedmemory erasing gunQuote"I can confirm: Not funny."[Source]Agent Jeff Trigger[2]is a government operative investigating the paranormal activity inGravity Falls, Oregon, alongside his partnerAgent Powers. Trigger and his partner, Agent Powers. In "Scary-oke," Agent Trigger and his partner Agent Powers both arrive at theMystery Shackto investigate mysterious activity in Gravity Falls, and begin an inspection of the shack's gift shop. During their visit, Powers discloses toDippertheir ongoing investigation of a paranormal case that he suspects is deeply rooted within the town, which entices Dipper to help with their research. Powers gives him a business card thatStanquickly confiscates, hurriedly convincing the agents that the paranormal aspects of the town are mere legends to drum up business.

characters.name: Alex Hirsch (character)
characters.description: Alex HirschBackground informationFirst appearance"Bottomless Pit!" (no lines)Last appearance"Weirdmageddon 3: Take Back The Falls" (no lines)InspirationAlex HirschCharacter informationOccupationCircusantCasino security guardAffiliationsUnknown circusCasinoHomeGravity Falls, OregonAlliesRob RenzettiEnemiesStan PinesBill CipherPowers and abilitiesJugglingUnicycling[Source]Alex Hirschis a resident ofGravity Falls, Oregon, a security guard in a casino and a circusant. Alex Hirsch appears in "Bottomless Pit!," on the TV, where he is seen juggling while riding a unicycle surrounded by crocodiles. He appears in "Soos and the Real Girl," as one of the guys that throwStanandGoldieout of the casino.

characters.name: America guy
characters.description: America guyBackground informationFirst appearanceIrrational TreasureLast appearanceWeirdmageddon 3: Take Back The Falls(no lines)Character informationHomeGravity Falls, OregonLikesAmericaQuote"USA! USA!"[Source]America guy[1]is a resident ofGravity Falls, Oregon. He is shown to be a very patriotic person. In "Irrational Treasure," whenMabeltries to pump up the crowd, she shouts "USA" and the man joins in, shedding several tears as he does so. In "The Love God," he attends theWoodstickFestival.

characters.name: Angry lady
characters.description: Angry ladyBackground informationFirst appearanceHeadhuntersLast appearanceGideon Rises(no lines)Character informationHomeGravity Falls, OregonEnemiesStan Pines[1]LikesGideon Gleeful(formerly)DislikesStan PinesGideon GleefulPowers and abilitiesStrength[2]Quote"Goodness!"[Source]Theangry lady[2]is a resident ofGravity Falls. The angry woman flattens a baseball. The angry lady is first seen in "Headhunters," amongst the crowd at the grand unveiling ofWax Stan. Like the other attendees, she is extremely disgruntled byStan Pineslying about there being free pizza for everyone who attended the ceremony, throwing and breaking chairs in a fit of anger. Later,Dipper PinesandMabel Pineslist her as a suspect for Wax Stan's "murder," and test her handedness, finding that she uses her right hand when she catches, and squishes, a baseball Mabel throws at her.

Later,Dipper PinesandMabel Pineslist her as a suspect for Wax Stan's "murder," and test her handedness, finding that she uses her right hand when she catches, and squishes, a baseball Mabel throws at her.

characters.name: Ascot and Dickie Sibling
characters.description: Ascot and Dickie SiblingBackground informationFirst appearanceGravity Falls: Lost LegendsInspirationThe Hardy BoysCharacter informationOther namesThe Sibling BrothersOccupationMeddling KidsGoalTo solve the mystery of the stolen gold chain fromPines PawnsHomeGlass Shard Beach, New JerseyEnemiesFord PinesStan PinesLikesFlavorless MaltsSayingAha!Finishing each others phrases.FateGoing to jail[Source]Ascot and Dickie Siblingare twins living inGlass Shard Beach, New Jersey. They are mystery hunters, and nicknamed themselves the Sibling Brothers. They were made famous by writer Jenkins W. Jenkins, who was an unemployed dockworker in Glass Shard Beach. He wrote his novels based on the exploits of Ascot and Dickie. The name of the main characters of the novels are also calledThe Sibling Brothers. Their hobbies as children were going to church and finishing each other's sentences. As adults, their hobbies are going to jail and finishing each other's life sentences.[1]

characters.name: Bat Boy
characters.description: Bat BoyBackground informationFirst appearanceGravity Falls Main Title ThemeInspirationBat BoyCreature informationSpeciesBat/human hybridEnvironmentU.S.A. (originally)Gravity Falls(in the TV series)Other namesBat kidHalf boy, half bat creature[Source]TheBat Boy, along with other images, is shown in themain title theme. It is one ofGravity Falls' oddities, although not seen anywhere except for the main title theme. The picture appears in the bottom right of the pictures. It falls down second to last. In theWeirdmageddonepisodes, it is replaced by aBill Cipher- Bat Boy. Bat Boy is in black and white. He is seen with his mouth open with eight sharp teeth, large, pointy, elvish ears, and a bald head. He has tiny arms with 3 fingers on each hand.

characters.name: Bats Biker
characters.description: Bats BikerBackground informationFirst appearanceHeadhuntersLast appearanceWeirdmageddon 3: Take Back The Falls(no lines)VoiceAlex HirschCharacter informationOther namesMan 16[1]OccupationBiker[2]GoalTo get a beautiful wifeHomeGravity Falls, OregonAlliesMabel PinesDipper PinesLikesFortune tellers[2]Love[2]Twins in costumes[3]Quote"But will she love me?"[Source]Bats Bikeris a resident ofGravity Falls, Oregonwho frequentsSkull Fracture. Dipper Pines He first appears in "Headhunters," in the biker bar Skull Fracture drinking, whereMabelsomehow strikes up a conversation with him. Mabel quickly wins him over with a paper fortune teller and tells him his wife will be pretty, but he worries if she will love him.

Beardy is a Manotaur Dipper called based on his brown beard. Dipper was unsure that was his correct name, but the Manotaur confirmed this. Beardy is a Manotaur Dipper called based on his brown beard. characters.name: BeeblyBoop's clerk
characters.description: BeeblyBoop's clerkBackground informationFirst appearanceSoos and the Real GirlVoiceKari WahlgrenInspirationManzi DeYoung[1][2]Character informationOccupationClerk atBeeblyBoop's VideogamesAffiliationsBeeblyBoop's VideogamesQuote"I'm not sure you want to buythat game,sir."[Source]BeeblyBoop's clerkis a young woman who works atBeeblyBoop's Videogames. In "Soos and the Real Girl," whenSoosexpresses a desire to buyRomance Academy 7to help him talk to girls, the clerk warns him that the game has been returned several times already, with the previous owner leaving a note that says, "destroy at all costs." After Soos demonstrates his social awkwardness further with a cardboard cutout,Mabelsays that they'll take their chances with it. The clerk is fair-skinned with long blonde hair and a cowlick. She has long eyelashes, black eyebrows and green eyes. She wears thick, square glasses, a pale blue shirt with rolled up sleeves, and a name tag.

characters.name: BeeblyBoop's clerk
characters.description: BeeblyBoop's clerkBackground informationFirst appearanceSoos and the Real GirlVoiceKari WahlgrenInspirationManzi DeYoung[1][2]Character informationOccupationClerk atBeeblyBoop's VideogamesAffiliationsBeeblyBoop's VideogamesQuote"I'm not sure you want to buythat game,sir."[Source]BeeblyBoop's clerkis a young woman who works atBeeblyBoop's Videogames. In "Soos and the Real Girl," whenSoosexpresses a desire to buyRomance Academy 7to help him talk to girls, the clerk warns him that the game has been returned several times already, with the previous owner leaving a note that says, "destroy at all costs." After Soos demonstrates his social awkwardness further with a cardboard cutout,Mabelsays that they'll take their chances with it. The clerk is fair-skinned with long blonde hair and a cowlick. She has long eyelashes, black eyebrows and green eyes. She wears thick, square glasses, a pale blue shirt with rolled up sleeves, and a name tag.

characters.name: Bigfoot
characters.description: BigfootBackground informationFirst appearanceThe Legend of the Gobblewonker(mentioned)Latest appearanceDreamscaperers(pictured)InspirationBigfootCreature informationEnvironmentGravity Falls ForestHomeGravity Falls, OregonQuote"Soos, be Bigfoot." --Dipper Pines[Source]Bigfoot,also known as theSasquatch, is a beast briefly shown inGravity Falls'opening creditsrunning and looking at the camera. It is an allusion to thefamous filmthat allegedly contains footage of Bigfoot. It appears in one frame of theshow's opening. In "The Legend of the Gobblewonker,"Soospretends to be Bigfoot as part ofDipper'sdemonstration on the importance of cameras.

Reality is an illusion, the universe is a hologram, buy gold, BYE![Source]Bill Cipheris a two-dimensional interdimensional dream demon from the now destroyed dimension,Euclydia. Formerly existent only in theMindscape, Bill briefly succeeded in gaining access to the real world and a physical form. He had been running amok inGravity Falls, Oregonsince being summoned byFord Pinesover thirty years ago. Known for his mysterious demeanor and sadistic humor, Bill is the main antagonist of the overallseries. ApportationClick "expand" for full list Clairvoyance(or "Ciphervoyance", as he calls it)Piano playingCross-dimensional awareness[1]Illusion manipulationIntangi
characters.personality_section: Bill is a cunning, blasphemous, eccentric, sadistic, psychopathic and physically irreverent being who finds most things amusing, particularly if they cause distress or harm to others. He is outrageous and outlandish, as well as a quick talker and thinker. Though he may come across as simply annoying, he shouldn't be underestimated; for when he is angered, he is a force to be reckoned with as he will unleash his near-omnipotent powers on those unfortunate enough to make him angry. When accused of being insane, Bill proudly agrees with the statement. He is also shown to be somewhat obnoxious, as seen when he makes his presence known to Dipper when the latter is trying to figure out a password within a limited amount of time. Occasionally, his voice tumbles to a lower pitch (usually when emphasizing a statement). As a demonic dealmaker, Bill is also shown to be a highly manipulative, very charismatic and charming conman being able to easily trick Ford Pines into believing that he was just a humble muse who simply wished to help benefit the human world by providing him with forbidden knowledge when in reality he was only ever using the researcher for his own purposes with the book smart Ford noting that the only person who could've possibly seen the ruse for what it was, was his much more street smart brotherStan Pines, who was himself an expert conman. Bill is not one who believes in rules. Instead, he follows his own selfish philosophy which means doing whatever he wants withou

characters.name: Billy's mother
characters.description: Billy's motherBackground informationFirst appearanceBoss MabelLast appearanceSoos and the Real Girl(no lines)Character informationHomeGravity Falls, OregonRelativesBilly(son)[1]LikesCoffee[2]DislikesBilly being injuredQuote"Billy! Your face, it's ruined!"[Source]Billy's motheris a resident ofGravity Falls, Oregon. She is an unnamed woman that first appears in the episodeBoss Mabelshe was with her son,Billyand quickly runs back to him because of his injury.  Billy's mother appears among thetouristsat theMystery Shackin "Boss Mabel." She angrily takes a refund afterBillyis injured byWendy's friends when they accidentally hit him in the face with a shrunken head, giving him a black eye and causing him to cry. She then storms out angrily with her son.

characters.name: Blendin Blandin
characters.description: Blendin BlandinBackground informationFirst appearanceNext Time On Gravity Falls(prototype)Tourist Trapped(cameo)The Time Traveler's Pig(proper)Last appearanceWeirdmageddon 2: Escape From Reality(cameo)VoiceJustin RoilandCharacter informationFull nameBlendin Blenjamin Blandin[1]Other namesThe Hairless man[2]Blendo[3]Blarblar[1][3]The Time Traveler GuyOccupationTime Anomaly Removal Crewoperative (former)[4]Inmate at theInfinetentiary(former)ClocksmithAffiliationsTime Anomaly Removal CrewGoalTo remove time anomaliesTo exact revenge on the Pines twins (former)Hide from the Time Paradox Avoidance Enforcement SquadronHomeYear 207̃012EnemiesDipper Pines(former)Mabel Pines(former)Waddles(former)Bill CipherLikesRusty Barrel ride at theMystery Fair[4]DislikesBeing under pressure[4]People touching histime machine[4]Being possessedPowers and abilitiesTime travelCamouflage watchQuote"It was after you stole my time device to win your stupid pig. I was cast out of the Time Anomaly Removal Crew, my whole life's purpose!"[Source]Blendin Blenjamin Blandin[1]is a supporting character inGravity Falls, being originally an antagonist. He is atime travelerfrom theyear 207̃012, who was sent to present-dayGravity Falls, Oregonto remove a series of time anomalies left behind byDipperandMabel Pinesduring the summer of 2012. The Time Traveler Guy After the twins' actions inadvertently cost him his job andincarceration, he swore vengeance on them, but has since made up with them and regained his post.[1]However, he was still picked on by other members of theTime Paradox Avoidance Enforcement Squadron, especially byTime Baby, which led him to make a deal withBill Cipherand allowed him to possess him and causedWeirdmageddon.

I'm Bobby Renzobbi! And what you need is a Huggy Wuvvy Tummy Bundle!"[1][Source]Bobby Renzobbiis a television spokesman who promotes a variety of odd products. He first appears in "Land Before Swine," advertising theHuggy Wuvvy Tummy Bundleand the "Arm Pants." In "Gideon Rises," he narrates an advertisement for theOwl Trowel.

characters.name: British dog man
characters.description: British dog manBackground informationFirst appearanceDreamscaperersVoiceKevin Michael Richardson[1]Creature informationSpeciesDogGoalTo scare SoosEnemiesSoosWeaponsWalking caneFateDisintegrated byDipper'slaser eye beamsQuote"'Ello, 'ello, 'ello! Who's crike for a schdig in the pudding?"[Source]TheBritish dog manis a being fromSoos'nightmares, brought to life byBill CipherinStan's mindscape. In "Dreamscaperers," after Bill Cipher establishes an arena shaped likeStan'shead forMabel, Soos,Xyler, and Crazto fight him upon, Soos unwittingly speaks about one of his nightmares, a "British dog man." This slip of the tongue leads Bill to create the dog man, who quickly decides to hit Soos repeatedly in the stomach with his walking cane. However, whenDippercomes along to help them in the fight against Bill, he destroys the dog man instantly with laser beams from his eyes. The dog man is an English Bulldog who wears a brown bowler hat, a brown suit, a light green tie, and a monocle on his right eye. He walks upright, like a human, and carries a walking cane. He speaks with a thick and stereotypical British accent.
